Fresh run · commit 306ac99 (Phase G.5)

Both ground-truth videos, rerun end-to-end

Same command, same deployed code, run separately on each video. Court/object detection is fully independent per video — no shared state, no per-video hardcoding.

Video 1 Badminton_video_example.mp4
Resolution 908×480 @ 30fps, 89.6s
Rallies / shots 6 rallies, 55 shots
Output CSV
output/Badminton_video_example_analysis_20260712_215248.csv
Video 2 Badminton_video_example_2.mp4
Resolution 854×480 @ 30fps, 176.6s
Rallies / shots 6 rallies, 82 shots
Output CSV
output/Badminton_video_example_2_analysis_20260712_215424.csv

Video 1

11 in-play frames, sampled across rallies 1, 3, 4, 5, 6.

Video 2

12 in-play frames, sampled across rallies 1–6 — different camera framing/resolution from video 1, same code, no per-video tuning.

Full annotated videos: output/debug_video/Badminton_video_example_annotated.mp4 and output/debug_video/Badminton_video_example_2_annotated.mp4. Both CSVs and all screenshots are saved locally under output/.
